EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A broken line of thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moving across the eastern Gulf of Mexico produce wind damage in Levy, Pinellas and Hillsborough counties, street flooding and a funnel cloud in Pasco County, structural fires due to lightning in Pinellas County, and a severe wind gust in Hillsborough County. Minor coastal flooding was briefly observed in Pinellas County in the wake of the line of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pinellas County 911 dispatch reported a fire due to lightning at a residence near St Petersburg between 20th Street and Forest Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong shortwave coupled with modest instability, strong shear, and steep mid level lapse rates resulted in several severe thunderstorms from western MA to eastern CT to RI to southeast MA. All the severe reports were large hail reports. The cool marine layer prevented any wind damage and tornado risk. The largest hail stone to fall was 2 inches in Narragansett, RI.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike caused a fire at a condominium complex near Carriage Ln in Duxbury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A passing cold front brought a round of strong storms through southern New England. Training storms moving over New Bedford caused flash flooding in addition to Providence, Rhode Island.  Earlier in the day it caused wind damage in Connecticut.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Acushnet, a house was hit by lightning causing damage to the home electrical system on Beaver Dam Rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of strong to occasionally severe thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moved eastward across the Florida panhandle and big bend during the afternoon of January 27th. While most wind gusts along the line were in the 40-50 mph range, isolated areas received slightly stronger winds with isolated wind damage reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Approximately 200 customers lost power in the Marianna area due to a lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of disturbances moved across the area resulting in numerous flash flood events that resulted in the flooding of many homes, extensive high water rescues, and a flood-related fatality.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a home fire in the St. Edmunds neighborhood off of FM 646.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system moved east across the area and led to the development of widespread showers and thunderstorms during the afternoon and evening hours of October 24th. A few of these storms become severe, producing hail up to quarter size and damaging wind gusts. A lightning strike near Keosauqua caused a fire that destroyed a historic church.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported that a historic church north of Keosauqua Iowa was struck by lightning that resulted in a fire and the total loss of the building. The time of the event was estimated using radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large thunderstorm complex resulted in numerous instances of damaging winds over much of the Mid-Atlantic during the evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a tree which split in half and fell onto power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought rain and wind to Northwest California a couple weeks following the major wind event on February 4th. This system was more convective and resulted in periodic wind gusts in excess of 58 mph on ridgetops across the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house shattering windows. No other damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cluster of severe thunderstorms moved through southern Missouri during the afternoon and evening hours on the 14th. A swath of damaging winds occurred along with intermittent large hail and one weak tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started a house fire on the south side of Rogersville. Significant damage occurred to the house.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level wave and surface boundary, paired with increased moisture advection and forcing over central into eastern Iowa resulted in scattered showers and storms, which tracked across eastern Iowa late evening March 29th into early March 30th. CAPE values just over 1000J/kg and weaker helicity values were favorable, which indicated the potential for hail with these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Delayed report of house fire in vicinity due to lightning. Time of lightning strike estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A supercell thunderstorm riding along a warm front produced a couple of tornadoes as well as straight-line wind damage over western Maryland.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a shed causing a fire in the 3600 block of Alternate US-40 National Pike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A supercell thunderstorm riding along a warm front produced a couple of tornadoes as well as straight-line wind damage over western Maryland.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused a fire that burned most of a house in the 19600 block of Westerly Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of increasing surface heating, elevated moisture, and increasing atmospheric life from an approaching mid level disturbance induced increasing storm development. The main focus of heavier rain was on the south shore of Lake Pontchartrain. Slow storm movement and moderate to heavy rain rates resulted in multiple areas of flash flooding across New Orleans Metro.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Report of a structure fire caused by lightning. Was passed along a video of the fire over social media showing the home on Alamosa Lane in Pearl River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms impacted Middle Tennessee during the late evening hours of March 14, 2024 and into the early morning hours of March 15th. The first round of thunderstorms were discrete when they moved into southwest Middle Tennessee. It was this first round of thunderstorms that were the strongest of this event with large hail and severe winds reported in Lawrence and Giles counties. The second round was a line of thunderstorms that weakened significantly as they crossed the Tennessee River, but not before causing wind damage in Wayne County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused an outbuilding to catch fire along Buffalo Road north of Lawrenceburg. There were no injuries. Damages are a rough estimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the region and caused a line of storms to push across the area and bring some isolated severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home and caused a house fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold pool aloft and surface front caused a round of strong to severe storms over all of southern New England. Hail from 1 to 2 inches fell in MA, RI, and CT while localized flash flooding was observed in southern RI and MA.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Harwich, lightning caused a structure fire on Seaport Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A few isolated diurnally driven thunderstorms developed the afternoon of the 29th. Some 1 to 1.25 inch hail was reported in the northern ATL metro, otherwise impacts were localized wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning strike caused fire at church near intersection of old mill rd near etowah village apartments.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another round of severe thunderstorms moved across the area resulting in numerous reports of wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person was struck by lightning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A motocross rider was struck by lightning during a race in Wise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 19-year-old male motocross rider was struck by lightning while racing at Oak Hill Raceway in Alvord. He survived with injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A small cluster of thunderstorms raced across southern Lake Michigan and came onshore, causing extensive tree damage from Michigan City northeast to Union Pier MI and then weakened with inland extent.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning strike at Autumn Lakes Apartments resulting in a large fire. 22 apartment units were damaged. No known injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of showers and thunderstorms developed in the early morning hours of February 10, 2024, ahead of a cold front in an environment that had very strong shear but little instability. The storms, mainly in southern Indiana and portions of north-central Kentucky, ended up morphing into a convective line that produced both wind damage and a couple of isolated tornadoes before weakening and pushing east of the region by sunrise. A total of two small, short-lived EF-1 tornadoes developed with the line, one in southern Indiana and one is central Kentucky.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started a structure fire, rupturing a gas line which also ignited. Cost to repair damages are unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Elevated thunderstorms produced many positive cloud-to-ground lightning strikes. One lightning strike caused significant damage in Montgomery County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike caused an electrical surge and significant damage in the basement of a house in the 21600 block of Gentry Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure brought multiple rounds of strong to severe thunderstorms to portions of central Iowa on Tuesday, April 16th, 2024. Elevated thunderstorms developed overnight and lingered into the morning across the area, remaining sub severe. This was followed by a second line of storms that lifted into the area by late morning. This line produced a few tornadoes in southwest to central Iowa. This was then followed by a third round of thunderstorms, more discrete in storm mode, that produced a few more tornadoes in northwest portions of Iowa. At least 8 short-lived tornadoes touched down, some causing damage in portions of central Iowa from late morning through the afternoon. A few of the severe thunderstorms also produced hail of 1 to 2 inches in diameter and wind gusts of 50-60 mph. Environmental winds behind the dry line were quite strong as well, occasionally gusting in excess of 50-55 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A small fire was started at a hotel in Pella after sustaining a lightning strike in the very early morning hours of April 16th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On May 29, a cold front moved the region bringing weak isolated thunderstorms. They caused minor damage from winds. A structure fire was caused by lightning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Observer reported out building structure fire caused by lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Multiple rounds of severe thunderstorms moved across southwest Alabama starting during the late evening hours of January 8th and continued through early morning hours of the 9th. This system produced four EF-0's and two EF-1's in Alabama.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house in Frankville and resulted in a house fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of storms from the previous day continued to roll through West Tennessee. There were a few reports of damaging wind before the line diminished.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A home on Show Boat Lane near Whitten Road was struck by lightning. This lightning strike resulted in a fire and destroyed the structure.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms formed along and head of an approaching pre-frontal trough and cold front in the early morning hours, some were severe and produced damaging winds and large hail. Behind these storms, an MCV moved through and produced more damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person injured when a building on Pine Street near Water Street in Lower Manhattan was struck by lightning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cluster of thunderstorms moved across portions of southeast Georgia during the evening hours, producing cloud to ground lightning strikes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Effingham County Fire Department reported a lightning strike at a home on Taylor Drive near Guyton. The lightning strike resulted in one injury and also caused a small fire in the attic.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two storm complexes moved across southeastern Mississippi, southwestern Alabama, and into the Florida Panhandle during the early morning hours of 5/10 producing destructive straight-line winds and multiple tornadoes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person injured due to a lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of disturbances moved across the area and produced flash flooding and severe thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Man was electrocuted while attempting to place a tarp over vehicles during a storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave and pre-frontal trough moved into a very hot, humid, and unstable airmass, which sparked off several severe thunderstorms, mainly producing damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man was struck and killed by lightning as he was returning to the beach to warn others about the impending thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On the morning of May 26, a stationary front had set up along the Ohio River just north of the state. During the course of the day, this front slowly lifted northward as a warm front, as a low pressure system began to develop and take shape just west of the state. This added forcing, combined with Kentucky's location in a warm/moist airmass, was enough to spark widespread showers and thunderstorms. WPC noted an outflow boundary on several surface analysis, the presence of which contributed to more shower and thunderstorm development. Most of the storms took place during the afternoon hours on the 26th, however, some storms and damage occurred as early as 3am EDT and as late as 2 and 3am EDT the following night. Amongst the many reports of thunderstorm wind damage, there was also a quick and small rotation that developed in the community of Corbin, which led to a EF-1 tornado, affecting a car dealership and several other businesses/homes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Video showed lightning striking a business in downtown London and starting a fire. The structure was reported a total loss.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms with lightning and damaging winds moved across central Missouri on June 29th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An apparent lightning strike hit a newly constructed home north of Linn Creek.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Weak forcing over the area was overcome by strong surface heating and modest surface moisture. Instability over Virginia was modest as well, but was enough for a few isolated afternoon thunderstorms, which ultimately produced areas of wind damage. In addition, a lightning strike damaged a power transformer, which led to power outages in the area of Martinsville.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a tree on Highland Street, and the tree was knocked down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the region and caused a line of storms to push across the area and bring some isolated severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home and caused a fire which resulted in some damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The late afternoon sea breeze and boundary mergers focused across the St. Johns River basin across downtown Jacksonville. Slow moving storms within a saturated airmass (precipitable water 2.18) produced locally heavy, flooding rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a natural gas line which |            caused a fire at 13319 Santorini Drive. The |            gas line was secured, and the fire |            extinguished as of 7:08 pm. There were no |            injuries. The time of the strike was based |            on lightning and radar data. The cost of damage was unknown but estimated for inclusion of the event in Storm Data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave trough pushed into the Mid-South during the early morning hours. A very moist and unstable air mass provided plenty of fuel for the trough to initiate convection. The atmosphere harnessed steep mid-level lapse rates, plentiful instability, around 50 kts of effective shear. and decent helicity. This type of atmosphere supported all weather hazards. Recent heavy rainfall events also heightened a flood threat. The severe threat continued into the next day as a line of storms rolled through just after midnight into the next day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home resulting in a house fire on Tumblebrook Cove.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late season storm brings heavy snow to the Sierra with widespread rain and scattered thunderstorms in the Valley. Periods of heavy snow saw major roadways such as I-80, SR-20, and US-50 close with chain controls also observed. There was a lightning strike fire which destroyed a mobile home in Newcastle.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike resulted in a mobile home fire around 6:15 pm PDT on Nob Hill Drive in the Castle City Mobile Home Park. The mobile home was destroyed. There were no injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: During the afternoon hours, scattered storms developed over Northwest Kansas and moved north-northeast. These storms produced mainly hail that ranged in size from pea to hen egg sized, with the largest occurring near Grinnell in northern Gove County. Most gusts associated with these storms were sub-severe; however, there was an isolated 61 mph gust near Norton, Kansas. A lightning strike started an oil tank battery on fire in southern Logan County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike started an oil tank battery on fire in southern Logan County. The fire burned for about an hour before being extinguished.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large cluster of thunderstorms developed near an inland advancing warm front in the morning hours along the southeast Georgia coast. These thunderstorms produced a few damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Chatham County Emergency Management Agency reported lightning struck a shed on Sandnettles Drive and caused a fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms occurred across the San Bernadino mountains and High Deset on May 15.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Earth Networks detected several dozen lightning strikes across the High Desert west of Victorville and across the San Bernardino mountains including Big Bear city and affect SR 38 and 18.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A man was injured when lightning struck his boat off of Cape Canaveral.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 57 year old man was injured and transported to the hospital when his boat was struck by lightning 2 miles off of the coast of Cape Canaveral.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moved across the mountains and plains producing gusty winds up to 65 mph, though mostly less than 55 mph. A lightning strike killed a 51 year old man while he was feeding his herd. In addition, 34 cattle were killed as well. Also, there were 3 water rescues involving 16 people on Dillon Reservoir due to gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike killed a 51 year old man while he was feeding his herd. In addition, 34 cattle were killed as well. Estimated that each cow cost $2,500.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A 7 year-old child was killed by a lightning strike on the evening of June 27.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 7 year-old girl was killed by a lightning strike outside of her home located between Bryan and Wickson Valley as a thunderstorm moved through the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A building was damaged from a late afternoon lightning strike.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A late afternoon lightning strike caused a fire at a building near the intersection of University Drive and Boyett Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous thunderstorms started to develop around midday in the vicinity of a southward moving cold front. As these thunderstorms tracked to the south through the afternoon, they produced numerous reports of large hail and damaging wind gusts across portions of southeast South Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Awendaw Fire Department reported a lightning strike at a residence. No fire was reported but there was minor damage to the home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brief round of thunderstorms moved through southern New England bringing scattered strong to damaging winds in western and central Massachusetts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Williamsburg there was a barn fire due to a lightning strike on Village Rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms fired up along a slow moving frontal boundary in an environment with 2000+ J/kg of SBCAPE and weak shear. Some storms became strong to severe and produced damaging wind gusts and large hail over southern CT.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: First responders reported a tree struck by lightning, injuring three people nearby. The EMS crew treated two people who were transported to area hospitals for further evaluation. The third person refused treatment. This took place at the TPC River Highlands golf course during a PGA Tour event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced severe tornadoes, large hail, and damaging wind gusts on the afternoon and evening of May 7. The largest hail stone reported was 4 inches in diameter.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ramona Skating Rink was struck by lightning, caught fire, then burned to the ground according to broadcast media.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A round of thunderstorms brought wind damage and flash flooding to Connecticut.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Stafford, lightning struck a house and damaged the chimney.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moved across central Pennsylvania during the early morning hours of May 8, 2024. Thunderstorms across the western highlands did bring some strong-to-severe thunderstorms producing sporadic wind damage as they continued to move across the southern tier of Pennsylvania.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning from a thunderstorm struck a three-story apartment building under construction, causing it to catch the third floor on fire for around 50 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow-moving front generated a round of thunderstorms which produced multiple areas of heavy rain and flooding from the Big Country to Central Texas on May 16.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A home on Yoakam Drive in Frisco suffered heavy damage from a lightning-started fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On the heels of the May 27th evening hail event, another batch of thunderstorms developed near the Red River during the overnight hours as a shortwave trough moved through the Plains. These storms eventually grew upscale into an organized complex, diving southeast early on the morning of May 28. An intense bow echo caused widespread wind damage from the northern parts of DFW, through the eastern parts of DFW and into East Texas. NWS Storm Surveys determined straightline winds of 80-95 mph caused some of the damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two homes on Moonseed Rd were struck by lightning. One suffered heavy damage, and other was described as moderate damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A teenager performing lawn maintenance was injured by a lightning strike in Altamonte Springs.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 17 year-old male from Altamonte Springs was struck by lightning while outside performing lawn maintenance at his neighbor's home. The victim stated in interviews with local media that rain had just started to fall at the time of the incident. The victim was transported to the hospital and made a full recovery within several days.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought locally heavy rain and a flash flood potential along with strong outflow gusts through Wednesday. Abundant moisture and instability were present as noted by the 0.81 PWAT reading from the 23 July 1200Z KREV sounding this morning. Sufficient clearing also helped temperatures to climb well above average for late July. Short term high-resolution HREF guidance showed an uptick in PWATs Wednesday as deeper moisture expanded northward across western NV and far northeast CA |as a ridge axis slid east with upper level southerly flow increasing ahead of a trough advancing into the Pacific Northwest. A few severe storms with gusts to 60 mph and nickel sized hail were forecasted on 23 July. But flash flooding would be the bigger concern as storms moved either slower or anchored near steep terrain and burn scars. For 24 July, mid level S/SW winds increased which increased both storm motions and shear for storm organization. SPC introduced a marginal risk for severe wind gusts near the Oregon border with similar severe weather threats for stronger outflows and blowing dust reaching the Basin and Range. While storms were expected to move faster, training storm  cells would continue to bring a heightened threat for flash flooding for areas south of I-80 with focus on the eastern Sierra into southern Lyon and Mineral counties. For 25 July, drier and more stable air confined storms to the Basin and Range where activity was forecast to be isolated but still able to produce gusty outflows and areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moving N-NE across the Basin and Range/Fallon area initiated a cloud to ground lightning caused fire in one the distillery's Rye fields that burned away 25% of the Rye crop. The fire was quickly extinguished by the local fire department and neighboring farm/ranch workers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Convection from a passing upper level trough and associated MCS in the morning brought an initial round of non-severe storms mainly in Minnesota into the far reaches of northern Iowa. However, severe weather potential increased by the afternoon as the aforementioned trough and surface boundary made its way into Iowa. Conditions ahead of the boundary included 40-50 knot values of 0-6 km bulk shear, along with 3000-4000J/kg of instability, 25-30 knots of 0-3 km bulk shear and DCAPE values around 1000-1500 J/kg. As a result, a line of showers and storms developed in the afternoon and passed over central Iowa into the evening, with a handful of hail reports in central Iowa generally from penny-size to 2 inches, while the greater majority of reports involved severe wind gusts of 55-70+ mph and associated damage. Tornadic activity within the line of storms did occur, with a tornado developing in the Des Moines Metro area, causing primarily significant tree damage, as well as some structural and power-pole damage in the area as well. Heavy rain given plenty of warm and moist air over the area also resulted in isolated flash flooding, specifically over Black Hawk county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management confirmed report of lightning strike at a house/barn located near Elkhart, which caused a fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow-moving front generated a round of thunderstorms which produced multiple areas of heavy rain and flooding from the Big Country to Central Texas on May 16.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media videos indicate a house fire was likely caused by lightning. The time was estimated by radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms developed shortly after midnight in the central Nebraska Panhandle. This activity moved east into more favorable moisture and shear, becoming severe during the early morning hours of June 1st. Hail up to golf ball size was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike killed one head of livestock at this location.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms occurred during the early morning hours of July 14th. In the East Jordan area, lightning struck a mobile home, setting it ablaze and resulting in a total loss of the structure. All occupants survived, though all were hospitalized for their injuries, including one occupant who was transported to a larger hospital for treatment.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cooperative observer reported lightning struck a trailer house, setting it ablaze, in the vicinity of East Jordan on the morning of July 14th. This report was corroborated by the Charlevoix Emergency Manager as well as Facebook reports. The structure was two trailer houses joined together; it was a total loss due to the fire. The occupants were all hospitalized...2 were taken to a nearby hospital in Petoskey where they were listed in fair condition; the third had to be transported to a larger hospital in Grand Rapids to receive treatment for his injuries for multiple days.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning from afternoon thunderstorms associated with the sea breeze causes 1 fatality and 3 injuries near St Petersburg in Pinellas County, and a structure fire near Palmetto in Manatee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media relayed report of a structure fire caused by lightning in Palmetto. Minor damage occured, however, there were no injuries reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning from afternoon thunderstorms associated with the sea breeze causes 1 fatality and 3 injuries near St Petersburg in Pinellas County, and a structure fire near Palmetto in Manatee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: County dispatch relayed report of 4 individuals that were struck by lightning and injured while standing under a tree near downtown St. Petersburg around 5:45 pm local time. Ensuing reports detailed that all 4 individuals were teenagers, 2 females and 1 male aged 16 along with a 17 year old male, and that one of the 16 year old girls had gone into cardiac arrest. Paramedics arriving on scene were able to restore the girl's pulse before taking her to Orlando Health Bayfront Hospital in St. Petersburg, along with one of the males that had received burns, and all 4 of the individuals were ultimately taken to Johns Hopkins All Children's Hospital for treatment and monitoring. Unfortunately, the 16 year old girl that had to be resuscitated on scene later succumbed to her injuries on August 19.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous thunderstorms developed across eastern and southeastern AZ during the afternoon hours. Deep and colliding outflows sparked new development across the lower deserts of south-central AZ during the early evening hours, including several thunderstorm clusters developing over the Phoenix metro area and producing strong to severe downburst winds. The environment that these thunderstorms developed under was characterized by MLCAPE values of over 1000 J/KG with DCAPE values in excess of 1500 J/KG, which were favorable parameters for severe downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cloud to ground lightning strike set a house on fire in the Laveen area in Phoenix. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of mid-level shortwaves approached an environment characterized by 1000-2000 J/kg of MLCAPE and marginal shear (mainly at or under 30 kt of Effective Bulk Shear). This allowed isolated severe thunderstorms to develop and produce damaging wind gusts across southeast NY.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: House struck by lightning in Katonah NY.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mesoscale convective system (MCS) moved into the Northeast and brought widespread showers and thunderstorms with it. With this system moving into the region in the afternoon and evening, daytime heating helped create an unstable environment. Enough shear was present to support long-lasting storms. In Northeastern Pennsylvania, there were no reports of tornadoes, but there were numerous reports of wind damage ranging from trees being knocked down to roofs being ripped off of buildings. A man, in his home, survived a lightning strike and only suffered minor injuries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man was struck by lightning in his home. Other than minor injuries, the man was alright.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy thunderstorm activity over Lee County during the early afternoon hours produced a lightning strike at the Lee County Flea Market in Smiths Station which resulted in a fatality.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike at the Lee County Flea Market in Smiths Station at approximately 12:45 PM CDT resulted in a fatality.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: During a concert following the Mud Bash at the Milan Dragway, a single lightning strike struck 4 individuals, who reported tingling sensations. Two of these individuals were transported to the hospital for further treatment and released.
Scattered severe thunderstorms in advance of a surface trough produced damaging winds and large hail across portions of northeast North Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a barn which resulted in a fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered severe thunderstorms well in advance of a cold front produced damaging winds across portions of central and south central Virginia.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tree was struck by lightning and caught fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of upper level disturbances moved across Southeast Texas early in the month of May causing multiple rounds of flooding and severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home causing a house fire on Pine Island Road. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnants of Hurricane Beryl tracked across the St. Lawrence Valley of NY and Canada during the afternoon and evening hours of July 10th. Numerous individual supercell-like thunderstorms developed and moved across NY into VT with various wind damage, hail and heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A structure fire was caused by a lightning strike according to emergency officials.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak trough moving across central GA was able to produce a line of thunderstorms across central GA. Two storms within the line were able to become strong with several trees down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Zebulon UMC caught fire due to lightning strike. Building is a total loss.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper low meandered over the region allowind scattered afternoon thunderstorm to develop. A couple became severe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home causing a fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A few thunderstorms developed along a stalled boundary. One storm produced a lightning strike that caused some damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cape May City Hall was struck by lightning Friday morning, causing damage to electronics inside the building and setting a pickup truck in the parking lot on fire. The lightning struck the VHF Antenna Tower. The facility was closed. Time given in report.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave trough moved into a moist and unstable environment, with high DCAPE over the region. Pulse convection developed, with downdrafts downing several trees and power lines across the state.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a transformer, knocking out power. Time estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A home in Owosso caught fire due to a lightning strike.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a power pole, with the attached electrical wire leading to a house causing a fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnants of Hurricane Beryl tracked across the St. Lawrence Valley of NY and Canada during the afternoon and evening hours of July 10th. Numerous individual supercell-like thunderstorms developed and moved across NY into VT with various wind damage and heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Structure fire determined to be caused by a lightning strike on a structure on Stony Hill Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A decaying derecho moved through the area late in the evening on July 15th. Storms raced eastward into southwest Lower Michigan and northern Indiana, prompting several Severe Thunderstorm Warnings for winds up to 70+ mph and Tornado Warnings for embedded rotation along the line. Three tornadoes were confirmed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local media confirms a small engine shop was destroyed by fire, likely caused by a lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused tree/powerline damage near Bellefontaine, MS.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning knocked a tree down, downing a utility line in the Bellefontaine community.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two lines of thunderstorms moved through Middle Tennessee on July 30, 2024. One of these lines moved through the area during the afternoon while the second arrived late in the evening. Damaging winds were the primary threat with these thunderstorms with numerous reports of downed trees and power lines received. Heavy rain was also reported across the Cumberland Plateau with flooding occurring in portions of Putnam and White counties. In Williamson County, a lightning strike caused a house fire which caused smoke inhalation for four firefighters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: House was stuck by lightning in Nolen Meade subdivision. Fire heavily damaged the attic and top floor. Four firefighters were injured due to smoke inhalation. Damages are a rough estimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep moisture profile and an increase of deep-layer shear over Central Alabama on July 28 resulted in very efficient rainfall occurring over central and northern parts of the region. Precipitable water values of 2.1 to 2.2 inches aided high instantaneous rain rates, at times on the order of 5-7 in/hr. Sporadic flooding occurred in Marion County during the early afternoon as two rounds of heavy rainfall moved through the area. By late afternoon, training thunderstorms over the Cahaba Heights area quickly flooded area homes and streets before another, much larger, round of thunderstorms overtook Jefferson County from the west. Many of the southern and western suburbs of Birmingham experienced flooding of homes and roadways during the early evening hours as 3-5 inches of rain fell across these areas.||The next day, an MCS tracking into northeast Alabama from Tennessee resulted in several trees being blown down in Cherokee County, especially the Centre and Cedar Bluff areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A residence along Rabbit Run sustained damage from a fire resulting from a lightning strike. The owner witnessed a lightning flash followed by a sparking TV and burning odor. No reported injuries. A lightning strike was confirmed via lightning network data and the residence was surveyed by the fire marshal.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A lead shortwave associated with an upper trough over the Desert Southwest helped initiate thunderstorms along and ahead of a dryline the afternoon and evening of April 1. Storms developed across the Big Country and spread east across North Texas. Several storms became severe and produced primarily large hail across several locations near and north of I-20 and along and west of I-35. The largest sized hail reported was Grapefruit sized (4.5) in Wise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started a house fire that resulted in a total loss of the house. The exact location of the house is unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Multiple rounds of thunderstorms occurred from Monday April 8 through Wednesday April 10 as a deep low pressure system moved east through the Rockies, eventually transitioning to an open trough as it traversed the Plains. The first round of thunderstorms began immediately after the region experienced a total solar eclipse! Large hail was the primary severe weather occurrence on April 8th, though flooding became a primary issue on the 9th and 10th following several rounds of showers and storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management reported a tank battery fire caused by a lightning strike near the intersection of FM 1417 and Davy Ln in Denison.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several reports of severe weather were received through the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fall River Rural Electric Cooperative reported a lightning strike knocked out power to several hundred customers in the areas of Idaho 33 and W 5000 S for about an hour near Victor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Historic flooding occurred on January 22nd. Heavy rainfall occurred between 8 am and noon across western San Diego county. The heaviest rainfall occurred between 9 am and 11 am in the east side of the city of San Diego in Encanto and Lemon Grove to Spring Valley. Rainfall rates were observed at 1.5 to 2.5 inches in 60 minutes and resulted in 1000 year return intervals. Over 200 water rescues at homes, streets and in water channels were performed by San Diego Fire and Rescue. FEMA found 1000 homes damaged in the following weeks and 600 were declared major damage resulting in up to 31 million in damage claims and displaced residents, including 1200 households, in temporary shelters for several months. San Diego County EOC was activated on January 22 and remained activated at a low level into May 2024. The areas flooded the most were Mountain View, Encanto, Valencia Park, Shelltown, Southcrest, and Lincoln Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a palm tree and started a small fire in Banning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture from the remains of Atlantic Tropical Storm Alberto wrapped around high pressure centered over the Desert Southwest the pulled up north behind it into central California on June 24 and 25. The result of this was thunderstorms which had little to no precipitation with them on both days. One cluster of thunderstorms produced dry lightning which ignited several wildfires east of Reedley during the afternoon of June 24 which became known as the Fresno Lightning Complex Fires.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dry lightning sparked several wildfires during the afternoon and evening of June 24 east of Reedley and Sanger. These fires became known as the Fresno Lightning Complex which resulted in several evacuations as the fires spread rapidly due to heavy fuel loading. The transmitter for KVPT Channel 18 was destroyed by one of the fires.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused some structural damage in the Myrtle Beach area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused an attic fire in a home on 33rd Avenue in Cherry Grove.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused some structural damage in the Myrtle Beach area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Media reported an apartment fire caused by lightning along Pinehurst Circle in Little River, SC.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Multiple rounds of thunderstorms occurred from Monday April 8 through Wednesday April 10 as a deep low pressure system moved east through the Rockies, eventually transitioning to an open trough as it traversed the Plains. The first round of thunderstorms began immediately after the region experienced a total solar eclipse! Large hail was the primary severe weather occurrence on April 8th, though flooding became a primary issue on the 9th and 10th following several rounds of showers and storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started a house fire in the 900 blk of Stonecrest Road in Argyle that heavily damaged the home, likely resulting in a total loss.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms along the Indiana, Michigan state line resulted in numerous reports of downed trees and powerlines around Elkhart, South Bend and surrounding communities.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flag pole stuck by lightning. Flag pole snapped.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture and an approaching trough axis fueled isolated thunderstorms over northern Mohave County. One storm produced lightning damage and hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused an outage of the phone system and also damaged a tree.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms developed ahead of a warm front during the late evening on Monday (6/24) producing large hail.  A Quasi-Linear Convective System developed across northwest Wisconsin and tracked southeast through northeastern Wisconsin during the overnight hours into early Tuesday morning (6/25).  The storms produced widespread straight-line wind damage, an isolated tornado, and some flash flooding across the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A barn was destroyed by a fire started by lightning in the town of Chase.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cluster of strong to severe storms moved across Chicagoland during the night of the 24th immediately followed by a line of storms which went on to impact northern Illinois through the morning of the 25th. The storms produced mostly wind damage, mainly to trees, as well as some marginally severe hail. The storms also resulted in one man being struck by lightning in Marengo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An employee of Nichols Farm and Orchard in Marengo was injured when he was struck by lightning. It's believed that lightning struck a tree and then traveled through the man who was nearby. He was transported to the hospital with minor to moderate injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid-level shortwave energy approaching the region along with a nearly stationary cold front across the area provided ample forcing within an environment displaying strong instability, deep moisture, ample shear and very strong downdraft CAPE, leading to strong and severe thunderstorms capable of producing large hail and damaging winds across Southeast South Carolina. A notable supercell thunderstorm developed near the Savannah River during the afternoon and tracked northeast through the Tri-County Area during early evening hours, producing a long and wide swath of damaging winds and large hail across inland portions of the Charleston Metro Area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a tree causing it to fall on a house along Fredericksburg Drive. Another lightning strike hit a tree causing it to lean onto a power line off of Myers Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers and thunderstorms impacted Berkshire County on Saturday, June 22. Isolated instances of thunderstorms becoming severe resulted in numerous instances of downed trees and wires with an isolated instance of a downed power pole and a tree downed on a house. Additionally, a barn fire was reported as a result of a lightning strike.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fire department reported a barn fire started by a lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered convection occurred throughout the day across the area as a weak cold front dropped slowly south across the area. By afternoon the front had largely stalled near the I-80 corridor and more vigorous convection developed along the front, fed by high instability exceeding 3000 J/kg and steep mid level lapse rates of 7 to 8 C/km. Storms first began near Newton and then back built towards the west along the boundary through the evening. Storms produced large hail and stretching resulted in a huge number of funnel clouds, including pictures with a least visible at the same time. Later in the evening a wave across Nebraska moved into Iowa, sweeping east along the instability gradient that the earlier evening convection developed on. A few damaging wind gusts occurred with this line of storms, however it remained sub severe most of the night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning strike resulted in a house fire. Home a total loss. Time estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid-level disturbance moved across the southern peninsula of Florida on June 30th, which led to abundant convective activity across the region. Scattered showers and thunderstorms developed across the eastern metropolitan area, especially Broward County, throughout the morning and early afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported that a 19-year-old male was killed by lightning strike as he was jogging at Vista View Park in Davie during a thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level disturbances interacted with leftover surface outflow boundaries from nocturnal MCSs and allowed for scattered strong to severe storms across the region each afternoon from June 1st to June 3rd. A combination of hail, damaging winds, and flash flooding occurred, but an EF-U tornado also occurred in Henderson County on the 3rd. One life was lost June 3rd in Henderson County due to a falling tree in straight-line winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started a fire in the attic of a home on Cross Timbers Drive in Keller.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Series of troughs enhanced afternoon convection during the week. Flooding, thunderstorm wind and hail reports were received at some point during the week. Activity caused a landslide in Bayamon. Lightning even struck the roof of a residence, causing injuries and damaged appliances, furniture, and the roof of the house.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the roof of a residence in Barrio Santana, Arecibo. The news report says that a 60-year-old woman was injured by debris from the roof and that her phone exploded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A summertime pattern was in place across the tri-state area with weak steering winds and high instability. The result was scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms with gusty winds and localized flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning resulted in a power outage near Sopchoppy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front dropped southward across Central Pennsylvania during the afternoon and evening hours of April 14, 2024, accompanied by a line of showers and storms. The storms developed in a relatively dry environment, with plentiful shear and adequate CAPE. Damaging winds were the primary issue, although there were two reports of quarter-sized hail. The storms weakened as they moved into southern Pennsylvania late in the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced lightning that struck a house on the 1200 block of Broad Street in Montoursville and caused damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front dropped southward across Central Pennsylvania during the afternoon and evening hours of April 14, 2024, accompanied by a line of showers and storms. The storms developed in a relatively dry environment, with plentiful shear and adequate CAPE. Damaging winds were the primary issue, although there were two reports of quarter-sized hail. The storms weakened as they moved into southern Pennsylvania late in the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning with a thunderstorm struck a tree near a residence on Rosemont Drive in Pine Grove Mills with a fire spreading onto the second floor of the home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary frontal boundary draped across central Illinois triggered scattered showers and thunderstorms during the night of March 31st into the pre-dawn hours of April 1st.  One of the cells produced a lightning strike that damaged a building near the Metamora Golf Course in Metamora in Woodford County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck and damaged a building near Metamora Fields Golf Club in Metamora in Woodford County.  A power outage also occurred due to the lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous thunderstorms developed in the early afternoon hours across southeast Georgia and moved toward the coast. A few of these thunderstorms became strong enough to produce gusty winds and cloud to ground lightning strikes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local broadcast media reported that lightning struck a car driving on Interstate 16 near exit 104, damaging the electrical system and disabling the vehicle. None of the passengers were injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed in the late afternoon hours across Beaufort County. These thunderstorms produced frequent cloud to ground lightning, with several lightning strikes to homes and other structures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike started a structure fire on Breakwater Court.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms lingered into the evening hours within a typical summertime environment. One storm became strong enough to produce damaging wind gusts and frequent lightning in Effingham County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Effingham County Emergency Management reported a lightning strike to a home in Guyton which caused a fire. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated to scattered thunderstorms developed across portions of southeast Georgia in the late afternoon hours. A couple of these thunderstorms became strong enough to produce gusty winds and cloud to ground lightning strikes. One storm interacted with a nearby mesoscale boundary and prompted a Tornado Warning across Chatham County. A funnel cloud was reported on two different occasions, but the storm did not produce any known damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Chatham County Emergency Management reported a house struck by lightning at N Leeds Gate Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated to scattered thunderstorms developed across portions of southeast Georgia in the late afternoon hours. A couple of these thunderstorms became strong enough to produce gusty winds and cloud to ground lightning strikes. One storm interacted with a nearby mesoscale boundary and prompted a Tornado Warning across Chatham County. A funnel cloud was reported on two different occasions, but the storm did not produce any known damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Chatham County Emergency Management reported that lightning struck a home on West Creighton Place.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a substation transformer in the 500 block of Long Circle Road in Cascade, VA. The strike caused loss of power to 3000 customers in the Brosville, Stoney Mill, Axton, and Cascade areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a substation transformer in the 500 block of Long Circle Road in Cascade, VA. The strike caused loss of power to 3000 customers in the Brosville, Stoney Mill, Axton, and Cascade areas. Damage values are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated to scattered thunderstorms developed across portions of southeast Georgia in the late afternoon hours. A couple of these thunderstorms became strong enough to produce gusty winds and cloud to ground lightning strikes. One storm interacted with a nearby mesoscale boundary and prompted a Tornado Warning across Chatham County. A funnel cloud was reported on two different occasions, but the storm did not produce any known damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Chatham County Emergency Management reported a tree on fire due to a lightning strike on Sandy Point Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm pushed across Saratoga County during the morning hours on April 30, 2024. One home in Greenfield Center was destroyed after a lightning strike struck a tree causing it to crush through the center portion of the mobile home.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One home in Greenfield Center was destroyed after a lightning strike struck a tree causing it to crush through the center portion of the mobile home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered storms developed across the region each day. Some gusty winds occurred from this activity and a few locations saw some downed trees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a transformer which caught the pole to catch fire. Power was out in a part of town.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A broken line of thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moving across the eastern Gulf of Mexico produce wind damage in Levy, Pinellas and Hillsborough counties, street flooding and a funnel cloud in Pasco County, structural fires due to lightning in Pinellas County, and a severe wind gust in Hillsborough County. Minor coastal flooding was briefly observed in Pinellas County in the wake of the line of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pinellas County 911 dispatch reported a fire due to lightning at a secondary structure behind a home on Treasure Island near Treasure Lane and Paradise Boulevard.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed in the late afternoon hours across Beaufort County. These thunderstorms produced frequent cloud to ground lightning, with several lightning strikes to homes and other structures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Beaufort County dispatch reported that lightning struck a home in Burton and started a fire. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed in the late afternoon hours across Beaufort County. These thunderstorms produced frequent cloud to ground lightning, with several lightning strikes to homes and other structures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike started a structure fire on Foxchase Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed in the late afternoon hours across Beaufort County. These thunderstorms produced frequent cloud to ground lightning, with several lightning strikes to homes and other structures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike started a structure fire on Lake Somerset Circle.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous thunderstorms developed in the early afternoon hours across southeast South Carolina and moved toward the coast. A few of these thunderstorms became strong enough to produce damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hilton Head Island Emergency Management reported four structures struck by lightning near the Harbour Town area. The lightning strikes occurred between 8 pm EST and 9 pm EST. A fire occurred at one of the structures.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front approaching central Pennsylvania in an environment of sufficient instability and shear allowed for thunderstorm formation during the late afternoon and evening hours, with multiple rounds of severe thunderstorms crossing the northern tier of Pennsylvania. The multiple rounds of heavy rainfall, coupled with local topography, allowed for flash flooding across hydrologically sensitive areas of Potter and Tioga counties. As thunderstorms progressed southward into the Lower Susquehanna Valley, they began to congeal into a line of thunderstorms producing more widespread wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced lightning that struck a utility pole, resulting in a fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As a surface frontal boundary pushed southward through the southeastern United States, winds began to veer south-southeast bringing moist, warm air into South Florida. This supported the development of numerous showers and thunderstorms across the area throughout the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of broadcast media reported that lightning struck a home on Marco Island, damaging the attic and leading to a subsequent fire. Time is based on National Lightning Detection Network data which showed several lightning strikes in the immediate area. Damage totals are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As a surface frontal boundary pushed southward through the southeastern United States, winds began to veer south-southeast bringing moist, warm air into South Florida. This supported the development of numerous showers and thunderstorms across the area throughout the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local media reported lightning damaging a home in West Park. The lightning struck the pavement outside the home, which then traveled up a metal fence and shot through the ceiling into a bedroom where a 1-year-old baby was sleeping, causing the roof to collapse. No injuries were reported. Time is based on National Lightning Detection Network data which showed several lightning strikes in the immediate area. Damage totals are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Record levels of monsoon moisture remained in place across NM as an upper level disturbance approached from the west and pushed the upper level high over the state south into New Mexico. Showers and thunderstorms developed over the high terrain in northeastern New Mexico midday June 26th, spreading over central and eastern NM during the afternoon and evening hours. These outflow boundaries produced severe wind gusts of up to 63 mph at Cannon AFB and at a mesonet station along I-40 in Cibola County. Additionally storms and several landspouts developed along a boundary in eastern Chaves County. A 91 mph wind gust was measured by a trained spotter just east of Hagerman, with damage being reported across town. There were at least two confirmed landspouts, one on the east side of town and another on the west side of town. Reports relayed to the Hagerman Police Chief indicate there could have been another landspout, but this is unable to be verified. After a lull in activity, overnight into the morning hours of June 27th, more showers and thunderstorms developed across the state by midday as the upper level disturbance moved across the state. These storms resulted in a severe thunderstorm wind gust of 63 mph at the Gallup Municipal Airport, flash flooding on U.S. Highway 64 at the Arizona border, and on NM Highway 605 just north of Milan in Cibola County. Storms moved east and gradually dissipated late in the day.||Key Words: Landspout, Thunderstorm Wind Gust, Flash Flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a tree in the front yard of a residence at 207 Linden Street in Hagerman, NM. The large and very alive tree was split down its main trunk with large branches causing damage to the roof of the house and their vehicle.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms impacted Middle Tennessee on September 1, 2024. Lightning with one of these thunderstorms reportedly struck a man near Antioch.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning reportedly struck and injured a 25-year-old man in the chest near Antioch. The man refused medical attention and exact injuries are unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The region was placed on the northeast edge of an upper level ridge, with subtle convectively induced disturbances moving through. Thunderstorms were triggered in an environment with weak shear and moderate to strong instability. Some of these storms became severe and produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four people injured by a lightning strike at a Voluntown campsite.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unsettled weather conditions prevailed across Puerto Rico through the first week of September as a retrograding upper level trough lingered in the region. The instability of the trough, diurnal heating, and low level moisture combined to generate widespread showers and thunderstorms across the region. Each day, accumulations ranged from 2 to 3 inches, with maximum amounts up to 5 inches in some areas. This led to several reports of mudslides, lightning, and gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the roof of a house in Residencial Fray Bartolome de las Casas in Santurce. Damage to the roof was visible from inside the house, with paint surrounding the area stripped away and small debris visible on the living room. The house was left without electrical power. Paramedics visited the scene, but everyone was ok.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unsettled weather conditions prevailed across Puerto Rico through the first week of September as a retrograding upper level trough lingered in the region. The instability of the trough, diurnal heating, and low level moisture combined to generate widespread showers and thunderstorms across the region. Each day, accumulations ranged from 2 to 3 inches, with maximum amounts up to 5 inches in some areas. This led to several reports of mudslides, lightning, and gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the outside wall of a house in Urbanizacion Mirador del Sol in Cabo Rojo. Damage was visible from outside the house, with paint and some cement surrounding the areas (entry and exit points were visible and showed damage) stripped away. Small debris was visible in the garden and driveway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed ahead of a cold front that moved eastward through Middle Tennessee on September 24, 2024. With plenty of instability and wind shear, these thunderstorms strengthened and became severe through the afternoon and evening. Damaging winds were the primary threat with numerous reports of downed trees and powerlines received. The most significant damage was reported in Ethridge where severe winds caused a tent to collapse, injuring three people. Some hail reports were received as well, but these were mostly less than one inch in diameter.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house on Kensington Park, causing a fire. Extent of damages is unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very deep tropical moisture associated with the remnants of Tropical Storm Alberto combined with monsoonal moisture was forced through eastern Utah and western Colorado due to a large high pressure ridge over the Central U.S. and a deep low pressure trough over the Great Basin. This deep moisture combined with strong instability and shear to produce widespread severe weather across eastern Utah and western Colorado with thunderstorms producing severe wind gusts, large hail and heavy rain which resulted in flash flooding and debris flows. One particular storm even produced a landspout tornado in southeast Utah. Frequent lightning also resulted in lightning damage in Montrose, Colorado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One of several strong to severe thunderstorms that passed through the city of Montrose produced a bolt of lightning that struck a house south of town. This then set the house on fire, eventually destroying it.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed ahead of a cold front that moved eastward through Middle Tennessee on September 24, 2024. With plenty of instability and wind shear, these thunderstorms strengthened and became severe through the afternoon and evening. Damaging winds were the primary threat with numerous reports of downed trees and powerlines received. The most significant damage was reported in Ethridge where severe winds caused a tent to collapse, injuring three people. Some hail reports were received as well, but these were mostly less than one inch in diameter.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house on Miles Court in Belle Meade, causing a fire. Extent of damages is unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed ahead of a cold front that moved eastward through Middle Tennessee on September 24, 2024. With plenty of instability and wind shear, these thunderstorms strengthened and became severe through the afternoon and evening. Damaging winds were the primary threat with numerous reports of downed trees and powerlines received. The most significant damage was reported in Ethridge where severe winds caused a tent to collapse, injuring three people. Some hail reports were received as well, but these were mostly less than one inch in diameter.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house on Belle Meade Boulevard in Belle Meade, causing a fire. Extent of damages is unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed ahead of a cold front that moved eastward through Middle Tennessee on September 24, 2024. With plenty of instability and wind shear, these thunderstorms strengthened and became severe through the afternoon and evening. Damaging winds were the primary threat with numerous reports of downed trees and powerlines received. The most significant damage was reported in Ethridge where severe winds caused a tent to collapse, injuring three people. Some hail reports were received as well, but these were mostly less than one inch in diameter.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Robertson County Emergency Operations Center was struck by lightning, causing minor damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon convection on May 27th over northwestern Puerto Rico produced cloud-to-ground lightning over Isabela, injuring three individuals.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A social media post indicated that a lightning strike affected 3 minors in Montones Beach in Isabela.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough lingered in the region, promoting unsettled weather conditions. Additionally, a tropical wave approached, bringing plenty of moisture in the region. Widespread showers and thunderstorms were observed across Puerto Rico, with rainfall accumulations of 1 to 3 inches each day, and maximum amounts up to 5 inches. This led to several reports of mudslides, lightning, and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning stroke a wall at Galeria 100 Shopping Center in Cabo Rojo. The pictures indicated a small hole was left on the wall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another tropical wave moved into the region by the middle of September. With previously saturated soils, and additional showers and thunderstorms, mudslides, gusty winds and flooding was reported in Puerto Rico. This event brought rainfall accumulations of 1 to 2 inches per day, especially in the interior and west, and portions of eastern Puerto Rico, including the San Juan metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A post in social media showed a lightning striking a palm tree at Balneario de Boqueron in Cabo Rojo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The proximity of an upper level trough, along with an area of enhanced moisture, very light winds and strong heating resulted in explosive convection developing over Puerto Rico. The thunderstorms produced very heavy rainfall, lightning, gusty winds, and even hail. Flooding and landslides were reported in several areas in Puerto Rico. Water surges along rivers were common, with some rivers even reaching flood stage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike hit the roof of a residence along PR-117, between km 9.8 and 10.4, in Barrio La Plata, Lajas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The proximity of an upper level trough, along with an area of enhanced moisture, very light winds and strong heating resulted in explosive convection developing over Puerto Rico. The thunderstorms produced very heavy rainfall, lightning, gusty winds, and even hail. Flooding and landslides were reported in several areas in Puerto Rico. Water surges along rivers were common, with some rivers even reaching flood stage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike hit a house in Calle Reina of Sector La Linea in Morovis, damaging part of the roof.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold low pressure passing overhead helped trigger isolated thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert. A couple of storms caused damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning set fire to a transformer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused an attic fire in Marquette during the evening of the 5th as reported by the Marquette Fire Department.  Nearby tree damage was also reported as the lightning strikes were confirmed by NLDN/GLD lightning data sets.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An attic fire reported by the Marquette Fire Department was likely started by lightning as determined by on-the-scene reports and NLDN/GLD lightning data sets.  No injuries were reported from the fire which occurred at a home on 310 Harrison Street in Marquette.  The City of Marquette Fire Department was assisted at the scene by the Marquette Township Fire Department, Chocolay Township Fire Department, the Marquette Police Department, UPHS EMS, SEMCO and the Maquette BLP.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over southwest into south central North Dakota in an environment of very strong instability and elevated deep layer shear. The atmosphere remained capped until early in the evening when rapid thunderstorm development occurred. The initial severe storm produced a tornado near Selfridge in Sioux County, which broke power poles. Based on the damage done this this tornado was rated EF-1. Additional storms developed ahead of a frontal boundary, producing large hail and damaging winds. One storm over southern Morton County south of St. Anthony produced a four-inch diameter hailstone. In the town of St. Anthony, wind driven hail produced extensive damage to structures, with the siding shredded on some homes. Later in the evening, a storm passed over and to the south of Sterling in Burleigh County, producing 100 mph straight line winds which dislodged a large grain bin, broke or severely damaged 15 power poles, and significantly damaged a large sunflower field. The storm then moved towards Steele in Kidder County where it produced a tornado just to the east of town. The tornado crossed the path of a stopped train and knocked about 40 railroad cars off the tracks. An oats field was destroyed and several hardwood trees were snapped. This tornado was rated EF-1. Additional storms then continued through the evening producing hail over south central and parts of north central North Dakota before moving to the east and dissipating. Along with the severe weather, three male teenagers were struck by lighting to the north of Sterling in Burleigh County while returning to their vehicle after goose hunting. All three were injured and required medical attention at a hospital emergency room, but were able to quickly recover from their injuries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A NWS meteorologist interviewed one of the victims. Three teenage males were goose hunting when thunderstorms approached their area. They packed up their equipment and were walking to their pickup truck close by when all three were struck by lighting. The victims were knocked down, and when they were able to get up they continued their return to the pickup truck and drove to a hospital emergency room in Bismarck.  One victim suffered a small brain bleed and reduced hearing. The second victim suffered a large cut in his hand along with a flash burn to his eyes. The third victim suffered tingling sensations in his right foot along with a headache. All the victims recovered from their injuries. The goose blinds they were carrying were severely damaged in the incident.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong to severe thunderstorms developed across various portions of Mississippi during the afternoons of August 14th, 16th, and 17th. These storms caused scattered wind damage from microbursts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused a house fire along County Road 265.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe storms produced damaging winds across much of central and southcentral Kansas during the evening and nighttime hours of August 14th. There were several reports of structural damage with wind gusts up to 80 mph reported for the strongest of these storms. Schools in the towns of Douglass and Sedgwick were cancelled the following day due to damage caused by these storms. These storms also brought frequent cloud-to-ground lightning, and a person was injured by a nearby lightning strike.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management relayed a report from emergency medical service crews of an individual transported to the hospital after a person's ear drum ruptured from a nearby lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought gusty winds to much of Utah and flash flooding to southern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike capsized a fishing boat with three passengers on board, throwing the passengers into the river, leading to one fatality and two injuries. First responders were called just after midnight local time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave trough over the Great Lakes interacting with a quasi-stationary boundary over eastern Ohio and western PA spawned severe weather from mid afternoon through late evening. MLCAPE near 2000 J/kg along with a relatively strong, elongated shear profiles led to an environment for strong to severe winds and possible tornadoes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A house was struck by lightning in Penn Township.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: More summertime thunderstorms developed across Middle Tennessee during the afternoon and evening hours of August 30, 2024. Gusty winds were the primary threat with these thunderstorms, and one particular storm in Manchester snapped a tree onto a powerline. Elsewhere, a man in Crossville was severely injured after being struck by lightning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning reportedly struck and severely injured a man worked in a tree along Wilson Road in Crossville. The man was air flighted to a Knoxville hospital due to internal burns and a broken vertebra. His injuries were not life threatening.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A lingering, small upper level short wave slowly departing the Midwest, combined with adequate daytime heating to promote scattered afternoon showers across much of central Indiana with a few embedded non-severe thunderstorms.|One thunderstorm crossing Jennings County produced a cloud-to-ground lightning strike that struck a tent in the County Squire Lakes neighborhood northwest of North Vernon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two persons inside the tent, an 18-year-old male and a juvenile female who was 9 months pregnant, were both injured.  Both victims were treated for their injuries, and were in stable yet serious condition as of the evening of August 3rd;  the baby was reported unharmed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pretty typical summertime convective setup was in place with a cold front decaying as it reached southern Louisiana and Mississippi while daytime highs were in the mid to upper 90s. Robust updrafts were abundant from high low level moisture, very warm surface temperatures and low level lift from the stalling frontal boundary. The resultant thunderstorms produced severe hail and damaging wind gusts. 2 homes were also damaged by lightning strikes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local broadcast media news story showed that a tree fell onto a house and car on Shadybrook Drive near Sherwood Forest Boulevard after being struck by lightning. No reports of injuries. Time was estimated by a combination of radar and lightning data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pretty typical summertime convective setup was in place with a cold front decaying as it reached southern Louisiana and Mississippi while daytime highs were in the mid to upper 90s. Robust updrafts were abundant from high low level moisture, very warm surface temperatures and low level lift from the stalling frontal boundary. The resultant thunderstorms produced severe hail and damaging wind gusts. 2 homes were also damaged by lightning strikes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Central Fire Department posted a picture on social media showing home on fire caused by a lightning strike in Central on Bryce Canyon Drive. Time estimated by radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered storms developed across central Virginia on August 26 under a belt of northwest flow aloft. A few storms became severe and produced damaging winds across portions of central Virginia.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast Media reported a fire caused by lightning at the Cardinal Elementary School.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered storms developed across central Virginia on August 26 under a belt of northwest flow aloft. A few storms became severe and produced damaging winds across portions of central Virginia.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast Media reported a lightning strike to a house in the 14300 Block of Charter Landing Drive near Midlothian High School that caused a fire in the attic. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered storms developed across central Virginia on August 26 under a belt of northwest flow aloft. A few storms became severe and produced damaging winds across portions of central Virginia.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast Media reported a lightning strike to a home in the 1700 Block of Bellows Drive off of Elkhardt Road which caused a house fire. No injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several reports of severe weather received.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a power pole resulting in a fire and part of a tree caught in wires.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several reports of severe weather received.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fremont County Emergency Manager reported lightning strike started a fire on the west side of Red Rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Northwesterly flow was present over Central Alabama with an area of low pressure over Indiana and a strong ridge centered over the southwestern United States. A residual outflow boundary present over North Alabama located to the southeast of a surface front over West Tennessee prompted convective initiation during the afternoon hours of August 2. Severe thunderstorms developed along a broken line around midday, which moved southeastward through eastern Central Alabama during the afternoon. Numerous reports of damage, mainly to trees, were received from Etowah, Calhoun, St. Clair, and Talladega counties. A second complex of storms developed west of I-65 by late afternoon, with more wind damage reports from parts of Tuscaloosa, Hale, and Jefferson counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local news media report of a home struck by lightning near the intersection of Pebble Ave and Winterhaven Dr in Alabaster. The fire department confirmed the strike hit a tree in the backyard, traveling to the home and hitting a gas meter, where a structural fire ensued.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed ahead of an upper level disturbance moving through the Ohio Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A person carrying an aluminum umbrella pole was struck by lightning at a park pool.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front was centered over the region and was the trigger for several showers and storms. Some of these storms produced damaging winds which brought down many trees. Lightning from one thunderstorm struck a tree, which caught on fire, in Campbell county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a tree which then caught on fire. Damage values are estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Ample monoon moisture and slow moving thunderstorms brought lightning, heavy rain, and flash flooding to a few locations across northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people were injured by lightning near the Rim Overlook at Horseshoe Bend.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread strong to severe thunderstorms developed along and behind of an approaching cold front during the morning of December 28th. Some of these storms produced small hail and a couple of the stronger clusters caused some wind damage. Later in the afternoon, increased instability and low-level wind shear in the warm sector allowed a few supercells to develop across Central and Northeast Louisiana. Immediately behind the supercells, a quasi-linear convective system developed and moved quickly eastward producing a few instances of strong damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning was reported to have struck an apartment building near the intersection of Millicent Way and East Bert Kouns Industrial Loop.
